title: Signals and Systems - Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter 1 on Signals and Systems. Problems cover signal classification (continuous/discrete, analog/digital, periodic/aperiodic, energy/power, causal/noncausal, deterministic/random), properties of bounded periodic and power signals, energy and power scaling under time compression, and periodicity of composite signals including determining fundamental periods and powers.

figures.caption: Transformed signal y(t) = -½x(-3t+2) and signal z(t) = ax(bt+c), with part (b) asking to determine v(t) such that z(t) is the running integral of v(t).
figures.description: The figure shows two continuous-time signal plots on horizontal time axes labeled t, with vertical amplitude axes marked by ticks including 3. On the left, the signal labeled y(t) = -1/2 x(-3t + 2) is zero except for a short positive rectangular segment followed by a linearly decreasing segment that reaches zero near t = 1. On the right, the signal labeled z(t) = a x(bt + c) starts at zero near t = -4, rises linearly to amplitude 3 by about t = 2, stays flat at 3 until about t = 8, and then drops abruptly to zero. Students should notice the time reversal/scaling and amplitude scaling indicated in y(t), and the ramp-to-constant shape of z(t) used in part (b) about a running integral v(t).
